title: Chopper Runs from Luffy
description: On pages 1–4, this part focuses on chopper runs from luffy, showing the key character choices and conflict behind this chapter beat.
scene: Chopper Runs from Luffy. The story turns inward to Chopper’s isolation, his fear of humans, and the painful memories behind his bond with Dr. Hiriluk. The scene description focuses on what the reader needs to understand before the dialogue continues.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Hmph. Can't even give me a decent workout...

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Kureha
dialogue.text: Yes, excellent job following my orders, zero!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: C-Come on then! We have to save Dalton!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: So... what's all this fuss about?

dialogue.speaker: Chopper
dialogue.text: Hurry up and dig him out!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: We'll explain later! In the meanwhile, we've gotta help with the digging!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: Everyone grab a shovel and dig!

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Kureha
dialogue.text: Ahhh! He trans- formed into an actual Reindeer! Aawesome!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #6
dialogue.text: I've got to warn the doctors right away!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #7
dialogue.text: Look! The castle's exactly as I left it!

dialogue.speaker: Wapol
dialogue.text: Now let us commence the revival of drum kingdom!

summary: This part highlights chopper runs from luffy and moves the chapter forward through its central emotional or plot turn.
